title: Writing - Part 2
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 140-190 words.

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice on a local events website:

'Have you visited a museum, gallery, or cultural attraction in your city recently? Write a review describing the experience, including what you saw, and whether you would recommend it to visitors.'

Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Review: The City History Museum — A Journey Through Time

Nestled in the heart of the old town, the City History Museum is one of those hidden gems that locals often overlook. I visited recently with a group of friends and came away genuinely impressed.

The museum is spread over three floors and covers the city's history from ancient Roman times to the present day. The exhibits are well-organised and clearly labelled, making them accessible even for visitors with little prior knowledge. The highlight was undoubtedly the interactive section on the medieval period, where you could try on replica armour and handle ancient tools. Children and adults alike were clearly delighted.

The staff were knowledgeable and enthusiastic, and free guided tours are offered every weekend.

If I were to suggest one improvement, it would be to extend the café's opening hours, as we arrived to find it had already closed at 3 p.m.

Overall, the City History Museum is an excellent and surprisingly entertaining attraction. Admission is affordable and the experience is well worth a few hours of your time.

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt:
A local council is deciding whether to build a new shopping centre in a park area on the edge of the city, or to improve public transport links instead. You have been asked to write a report presenting the advantages and disadvantages of each option and making a recommendation.

Write your report.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Report: Shopping Centre vs. Public Transport Improvements in Greenfield

Introduction
This report examines two proposals for improving life in Greenfield and recommends the most suitable option based on community needs.

Option 1: New Shopping Centre
Building a shopping centre in the park would create approximately 300 local jobs and boost the local economy. Residents would benefit from convenient access to a wider range of shops. However, the loss of green space would be deeply unpopular with residents, and the increased traffic could worsen existing congestion problems.

Option 2: Improved Public Transport
Investing in better bus and tram links would benefit a far greater number of residents, particularly those without cars. It would also reduce traffic and carbon emissions. The main disadvantage is that improvements take time to implement and the short-term economic impact would be limited.

Recommendation
Having considered both options, I would strongly recommend improving public transport. It addresses a long-standing community need, benefits the greatest number of people, and supports environmental goals. Green spaces, once lost, are very difficult to recover.

Conclusion
The council should prioritise public transport investment as the more sustainable and inclusive choice.

tasks.title: Letter
tasks.prompt:
You recently moved from a small town to a large city. Your friend Rosa, who is considering making a similar move, has asked you to write a letter sharing your experience. Describe the main differences between city and town life, explain what you enjoy and what you find difficult, and give your honest advice.

Write your letter.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear Rosa,

I was so pleased to hear from you! Since you're thinking about moving to the city, let me share what life has been like for me over the past few months.

The most obvious difference is the pace of life. Everything here moves much faster — the streets are busier, people seem more focused on their own routines, and there is always something happening. At first, I found this exciting, but I'll admit that the constant noise and crowds can be exhausting.

On the positive side, the opportunities here are extraordinary. There are dozens of theatres, galleries, restaurants, and events every week. Career-wise, I have already applied for three internships that simply would not have been available back home.

However, I do miss the sense of community that our town had. In the city, I barely know my neighbours. Building a social life takes real effort here.

My honest advice? Come with realistic expectations. The city is full of opportunities, but also full of challenges. If you are prepared to work for it, the experience will absolutely be worth it.

Hope to see you soon!
Yours,
Luisa
